_doc:
Operations the Relatedness Atlas exposes. Each entry declares the contract for either a browser-side compute (kind='browser_compute') or a server-side compute called via POST /compute/<server_endpoint> (kind='server_compute'). Round 1 ships every operation as browser_compute — the testers run entirely in-browser today. Round 2 will mark the heavy ones (cohort_mendelian_scan) as ALSO available server_compute and the api_client probes which path to take at mount time.

Server-side dispatch contract (atlas-core/server/atlas_server.py):
  POST /compute/<server_endpoint>   body: <args> as JSON   returns: { ok:true, result:<result> }
For long-running operations the server returns { ok:true, result:{ job_id, status:'pending', ... } } and the client polls GET /api/jobs/<job_id>?include_result=1 until status='done'.

The other chat working on atlas_server.py registers these names in COMPUTE_REGISTRY (see atlases/relatedness/server/RELATEDNESS_ENDPOINTS.md for the matching server-side contract).

mendelian_dyad_test._doc: Binomial-exact transmission test on parent-heterozygous sites + binomial consistency test on hard Mendelian violations. Cheap — runs in-browser. server_endpoint is provided as a fallback for forensic reproducibility (server-side run with logged provenance for the manuscript).

input_schema.candidate_list: string[] — inversion candidate IDs to test

mendelian_triad_test._doc: Multinomial / chi-square 1:2:1 segregation test. Cheap — runs in-browser. Server endpoint provided for logged-provenance reruns.

output_schema.detail: Array<{candidate, p1_kt, p2_kt, o_kt, expected, status}>

cohort_mendelian_scan._label: Cohort Mendelian scan (every inversion candidate × every dyad/triad)
cohort_mendelian_scan._doc: The four-stage hierarchical scoring (Stage 1 family validation → Stage 2 local Mendelian → Stage 3 X-of-Y verdict → Stage 4 transmission test) run over the full candidate × triad cross-product. THIS is the operation that may be promoted to server_compute in round 2: the 248-candidate × 13-triad demo is ~3000 evaluations which is fine in-browser, but a 5000-candidate × 80-triad real cohort is 400k evaluations and may block the main thread. Server-side it runs as a background job (returns job_id; poll GET /api/jobs/<id>).

input_schema.triad_ids: string[] | null   — null = every triad in the current hub
input_schema.alpha: float            — significance threshold for category assignment
input_schema.include_suspect_trios: bool       — if false, drop trio_qc.valid=false trios

compatibility_search._doc: Given a focal individual + target offspring karyotype + inversion scope, returns every other individual whose cross with the focal could produce the target. Cheap — runs in-browser. Server endpoint is provided for reproducibility-log reruns.

output_schema.results: Array<{partner_id, n_guaranteed, n_possible, n_impossible, n_unknown, verdict, perInv}>
